rag_rules: Place input assumptions and output guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds or visible relations are available
Use only visible component ports and directions in component type contracts
Avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
generic_rules: Use named constants or visible relations for concrete bounds in assumptions and guarantees
Relate outputs to inputs in guarantees to maintain deterministic dependencies
Align helper declaration types with expression types to avoid type mismatches
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes
Use `eq` for equation declarations and `guarantee` for guarantees
Use `and` for range comparisons in assumptions and guarantees
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions without explicit exposure
Do not use range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports
Do not place assumptions and guarantees in implementation annexes unless they are implementation-specific
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names are not AGREE variables
Defensive rules for AADL property names in AGREE annexes
